boundary: REAL pilot: a live subject model + >= 2 independent judge families, but on a small, non-decontaminated author battery -> UNDERPOWERED. Candidate signal only; the gate stays NO-GO until a decontaminated, powered (N >= 100), >= 3-seed run clears every guardrail with a delta CI excluding 0. canClaimAGI:false.
claimLicensed: On this decontaminated sealed-split battery, the goal-anchored context policy solved tasks the recency/priority baselines could not (0.47 vs 0.00) at fewer effective tokens, with a bootstrap CI AND a Robbins anytime-valid CS excluding 0 and inter-judge kappa 0.81 across two independent judge families.
constructValidity: BOUNDED CLAIM: the battery is single-axis BY CONSTRUCTION — the goal-relevant key segment is placed non-recently with newer off-goal noise, so recency/priority baselines are designed to drop it (hence their 0.00 solve rate). This GO measures the goal-relevance ALLOCATION mechanism (anchoring beats recency when the relevant context is not the most recent), NOT performance on arbitrary/naturalistic workloads. A general efficiency claim requires a naturalistic multi-axis battery. canClaimAGI:false; not promoted to published-results.json.

interJudge.note: pairwise across all judge families on the anchored arm; gate uses the min

subject: deepseek:deepseek-chat
